400	def extractability(text: str) -> dict:
401	    """Are these bytes even a readable rendering of a document? Derived, never asserted."""
402	    toks = _WORDY.findall(text.lower())
403	    n = len(toks) or 1
404	    sw = sum(1 for t in toks if t in _FUNCTION_WORDS) / n
405	    words = text.split() or ['']
406	    cid = len(_CID.findall(text)) / len(words)
407	    alpha = sum(c.isalpha() or c.isspace() for c in text) / max(1, len(text))
408	    if sw < CORRUPT_STOPWORD_DENSITY:
409	        verdict = 'CORRUPT'          # not English. A cipher, a glyph dump, or not prose at all.
410	    elif cid > DEGRADED_CID_RATIO:
411	        verdict = 'DEGRADED'         # readable body, but a non-embedded font ate part of it
412	    else:
413	        verdict = 'CLEAN'
414	    return dict(verdict=verdict, stopword_density=round(sw, 4),
415	                cid_ratio=round(cid, 4), alpha_ratio=round(alpha, 3))
416	
417	
418	# A body that is the paper CONTAINS THE PAPER'S OWN ABSTRACT'S VOCABULARY -- the abstract is a
419	# summary of it. Measured on this corpus: every genuine body scores 0.975-1.000 (7 of 9 at exactly
420	# 1.000). The one body that is a DIFFERENT PAPER scores 0.391. The threshold below sits in the middle
421	# of that empty gap; __main__ reprints the distribution so it can be re-derived rather than believed.
422	CONTRADICTED_VOCAB_OVERLAP = 0.70
423	_IDENT_MIN_ABSTRACT_WORDS = 40      # below this the abstract is too thin to be a probe
424	_IDENT_MIN_VOCAB = 10
425	_IDENT_MIN_BODY_WORDS = 1000        # below this the body is a stub; absence proves nothing
426	
427	
428	def identity_agreement(text: str, work: Work, independent_abstract: str = '',
429	                       readable: bool = True) -> dict:
430	    """Do these bytes belong to the Work the row claims? THE CHECK THAT CATCHES A DIFFERENT PAPER.
431	
432	    Not one component asked this. A title matcher matched the phrase "Rise of the Machines" and
433	    handed us a MATHEMATICS PREPRINT; every downstream check then verified, correctly and
434	    irrelevantly, that the span was verbatim IN THE MATHEMATICS PREPRINT.
435	
436	    CONTRADICTED REQUIRES POSITIVE EVIDENCE OF A DIFFERENT WORK. It is never inferred from the
437	    ABSENCE of ours. My first version of this function inferred it from absence -- "none of the
438	    claimed authors appears in the text" -- and it convicted EIGHT papers, all innocent: seven were
439	    bare abstracts that simply do not carry an author block, and the eighth was Autor (2003), whose
440	    title page is (cid:N) font codes. Meanwhile the ONE genuinely foreign paper scored CONFIRMED,
441	    because its two-word title ("Rise of the Machines") appears verbatim in the maths paper.
442	    Absence of our evidence, read as evidence of absence, is the HTTP-429-means-"no free copy exists"
443	    error exactly. So the only route to CONTRADICTED is a positive, two-sided content test:
444	
445	        the row carries an abstract FROM THE BIBLIOGRAPHY (independent of whatever we fetched);
446	        the body we fetched does NOT contain that abstract's distinctive vocabulary;
447	        therefore the body is not a rendering of this work.
448	    """
449	    head = text[:20000]
450	    authors = [a for a in (work.authors or []) if len(a) > 2]
451	    named = [a for a in authors if re.search(r'\b' + re.escape(a) + r'\b', head, re.I)]
452	    twords = {w for w in re.findall(r'[a-z]{5,}', (work.title or '').lower())}
453	    tfound = {w for w in twords if w in head.lower()}
454	    # A title of one or two distinctive words is not a test: "Rise of the Machines" yields {machines},
455	    # which the maths paper contains. Below three, the title signal is UNUSABLE, not passing.
456	    title_usable = len(twords) >= 3
457	
458	    overlap: float | None = None
459	    vocab = {w for w in re.findall(r'[a-z]{5,}', (independent_abstract or '').lower())
460	             if w not in _FUNCTION_WORDS}
461	    if (len((independent_abstract or '').split()) >= _IDENT_MIN_ABSTRACT_WORDS
462	            and len(vocab) >= _IDENT_MIN_VOCAB
463	            and len(text.split()) >= _IDENT_MIN_BODY_WORDS):
464	        low = text.lower()
465	        overlap = round(sum(1 for w in vocab if w in low) / len(vocab), 3)
466	
467	    if not readable:
468	        # You cannot judge identity from bytes you cannot read. Autor (2003)'s authors and title are
469	        # absent because they are (cid:11)(cid:3)... -- that is a fact about the FONT, not the paper.
470	        verdict, basis = 'UNRESOLVED', 'bytes are not readable — identity cannot be judged'
471	    elif overlap is not None and overlap < CONTRADICTED_VOCAB_OVERLAP:
472	        verdict = 'CONTRADICTED'
473	        basis = (f'the body contains only {overlap:.0%} of the distinctive vocabulary of this work’s '
474	                 f'own abstract (genuine bodies on this corpus: 97.5–100%) — THESE ARE NOT ITS BYTES')
475	    elif named:
476	        verdict, basis = 'CONFIRMED', f'{len(named)}/{len(authors)} claimed authors named in the text'
477	    elif overlap is not None and overlap >= 0.90:
478	        verdict, basis = 'CONFIRMED', f'body contains {overlap:.0%} of its own abstract’s vocabulary'
479	    elif title_usable and len(tfound) >= 0.8 * len(twords):
480	        verdict, basis = 'CONFIRMED', f'{len(tfound)}/{len(twords)} distinctive title words present'
481	    else:
482	        verdict, basis = 'UNRESOLVED', 'no positive evidence either way (absence proves nothing)'
483	
484	    return dict(verdict=verdict, basis=basis,
485	                authors_named=len(named), authors_claimed=len(authors),
486	                title_words_found=len(tfound), title_words=len(twords), title_usable=title_usable,
487	                abstract_vocab_overlap=overlap)
488	
489	
490	def derive_expression_kind(text: str, work_kind: str = 'study') -> tuple[str, str]:
491	    """Which VERSION do these bytes say they are? Derived from self-identifying furniture.
492	
493	    Returns ('unknown', ...) freely. `unknown` is the honest answer for a PDF body with its title
494	    page mangled, and an honest `unknown` costs us evidence -- which is the correct price.
495	
496	    THE SCHOLARLY VERSION TAXONOMY APPLIES ONLY TO SCHOLARLY WORKS. There is no preprint of a statute
497	    and no accepted manuscript of a judicial opinion; the authoritative text IS the artifact, and the
498	    registry says so. Running the NBER/arXiv/"accepted manuscript" regexes over a court opinion does
499	    not find nothing — it finds whatever the opinion happens to QUOTE, which is how a whole-text scan
500	    for "NBER Working Paper" once read the bibliography and called the JEP article a working paper.
501	    """
502	    fam = WORK_KIND_ARTIFACT.get(work_kind or 'study')
503	    if fam is not None:
504	        ex = KIND_PROFILE[fam]['expression']
505	        return ex, (f'work kind `{work_kind}` — the version taxonomy of a {fam.replace("_", " ")} is '
506	                    f'not the scholarly one; these bytes are its {ex.replace("_", " ")}')
507	    head = text[:12000]
508	    wp, pre, am, jr = (_WP_MARK.search(head), _PREPRINT_MARK.search(head),
509	                       _AM_MARK.search(head), _JOURNAL_MARK.search(head))
510	    # Order matters: an accepted manuscript deposited in a repository carries the journal's citation
511	    # block too, so the AM marker must be read BEFORE the journal marker or every AM reads as typeset.
512	    if am:
513	        return 'accepted_manuscript', f'bytes say: {am.group(0)[:48]!r}'
514	    if wp:
515	        return 'working_paper', f'bytes say: {wp.group(0)[:48]!r}'
516	    if pre:
517	        return 'preprint', f'bytes say: {pre.group(0)[:48]!r}'
518	    if jr:
519	        return 'journal_version', f'typeset journal furniture: {jr.group(0)[:48]!r}'
520	    return 'unknown', 'no self-identifying version furniture in the first 12,000 chars'
521	
522	
523	def profile(text: str, work: Work, independent_abstract: str = '',
524	            fetch_outcome: str = 'ok') -> dict:
525	    """THE CONTENT-DERIVED ARTIFACT PROFILE. Replaces MIN_WORDS entirely.
526	
527	    Every field is derived FROM THE BYTES. `complete` is judged against the profile FOR THIS KIND,
528	    so a short judicial opinion is complete and a 535-word cookie banner is not.
529	    """
530	    text = text or ''
531	    words = len(text.split())
532	    ex = extractability(text)
533	    ident = identity_agreement(text, work, independent_abstract, readable=ex['verdict'] == 'CLEAN')
534	
535	    view = View(text)
536	    secs = sections_of(view)
537	    present = sorted({name for _, _, name in secs if name not in ('front', 'body')})
538	    result_secs = [s for s in present if s in RESULT_BEARING]   # REPORTED. GATES NOTHING. See above.
539	    # body/chrome ratio, measured on the bytes -- not on a word count.
540	    chrome_hits = len(_CHROME.findall(text))
541	    body_chars = sum(e - s for s, e, name in secs if SECTION_WEIGHT.get(name, 0.5) > 0)
542	    body_ratio = round(body_chars / max(1, len(text)), 3)
543	    chrome_per_kw = round(1000 * chrome_hits / max(1, words), 2)
544	
545	    work_kind = work.kind or 'study'
546	    family = WORK_KIND_ARTIFACT.get(work_kind)      # None => scholarly; else the kind is FIXED by the work
547	    ekind, ebasis = derive_expression_kind(text, work_kind=work_kind)
548	
549	    # ---- artifact_kind: DERIVED, in strict order of what the bytes can actually PROVE ------------
550	    # THE BYTES MAY ALWAYS DEMOTE. They may never promote. A judicial opinion whose fetch returned a
551	    # login wall is a landing_page, not a short opinion — so the demotions run FIRST, above the work's
552	    # own kind. But nothing in a stranger's letterhead can turn a statute into a working paper, so the
553	    # work's kind runs ABOVE the scholarly version furniture.
554	    if fetch_outcome != 'ok':
555	        # A failed fetch is a fact about our REQUEST, never a fact about the world.
556	        kind, basis = 'unknown', f'fetch outcome: {fetch_outcome}'
557	    elif ident['verdict'] == 'CONTRADICTED':
558	        # The single most important branch in this file. These bytes are SOMEONE ELSE'S PAPER.
559	        kind, basis = 'wrong_work', ident['basis']
560	    elif ex['verdict'] == 'CORRUPT':
561	        kind, basis = 'extraction_failure', (
562	            f"stopword density {ex['stopword_density']} < {CORRUPT_STOPWORD_DENSITY} — "
563	            f"these bytes are not English prose")
564	    elif chrome_hits >= 2 and (chrome_per_kw >= 5 or (family is None and words < SCHOLARLY_STUB_FLOOR)):
565	        # WEB FURNITURE WITH NO DOCUMENT UNDER IT. Note that the AEA landing page for Autor (2015)
566	        # carries the article's own "vol. 29, no. 3" citation block -- so journal furniture proves
567	        # only that a page CITES the article, never that it IS the article. This test runs first for
568	        # exactly that reason.
569	        # The LENGTH half of this test is scholarly-only: a 300-word statute section is a statute
570	        # section, and only the chrome DENSITY may call it a web page.
571	        kind, basis = 'landing_page', (
572	            f'{chrome_hits} web-chrome markers ({chrome_per_kw}/1k words) over {words} words — '
573	            f'this is a web page about the document, not the document')
574	    elif family is not None:
575	        # A CASE, A STATUTE, A TRIAL RECORD. Its completeness is not a word count and never was.
576	        kind, basis = family, (f'work kind `{work_kind}` — this is its {family.replace("_", " ")}, '
577	                               f'which is COMPLETE AT ANY LENGTH (registry stub_floor=None)')
578	    elif ekind == 'journal_version':
579	        kind, basis = 'journal_article', ebasis
580	    elif ekind == 'proceedings_version':
581	        kind, basis = 'proceedings_paper', ebasis
582	    elif ekind in ('working_paper', 'preprint', 'accepted_manuscript'):
583	        kind, basis = ekind, ebasis
584	    elif words < SCHOLARLY_ABSTRACT_FLOOR:
585	        kind, basis = 'citation_only', f'{words} words — a citation stub, not even an abstract'
586	    elif words < SCHOLARLY_STUB_FLOOR:
587	        kind, basis = 'abstract', f'{words} words with no version furniture — a stub, not the article'
588	    else:
589	        kind, basis = 'unknown', ebasis
590	
591	    # ---- completeness: THE ONE REDUCER, driven by the registry FOR THIS KIND ---------------------
592	    complete, reasons = judge_completeness(kind, words, ex['verdict'])
593	
594	    return dict(
595	        artifact_kind=kind, artifact_kind_basis=basis,
596	        expression_kind=ekind, expression_kind_basis=ebasis,
597	        complete=complete, incomplete_because=reasons,
598	        n_words=words, sections_present=present, result_bearing_sections=result_secs,
599	        body_ratio=body_ratio, chrome_markers=chrome_hits, chrome_per_1k_words=chrome_per_kw,
600	        extractability=ex, identity=ident, fetch_outcome=fetch_outcome,
601	    )
602	
603	
604	# =================================================================================================
605	# 3. THE GRAPH
606	# =================================================================================================
607	
608	class SpanBindingError(ValueError):
609	    """A span that cannot be bound to real bytes. Not a warning: THE BINDING DOES NOT EXIST."""
610	
611	
612	class GraphIntegrityError(ValueError):
613	    """A graph on disk that does not agree with itself. It is REFUSED, never repaired."""
614	
615	
616	# What it takes to ASSERT that a span survives from one expression into another. Only a claim about
617	# BYTES can do it -- that is the entire content of the word "span-preserving".
618	_AUTHENTICATES = re.compile(
619	    r'byte[- ]for[- ]byte|byte[- ]level|checksum|sha-?256|content[_ -]hash'
620	    r'|identical (?:bytes|text|spans?)|span[- ]level (?:diff|comparison)'
621	    r'|(?:diffed|compared) (?:it )?against the .{0,30}(?:pdf|bytes|text|version)', re.I)
622	_PROVES_NOTHING_ABOUT_BYTES = re.compile(
623	    r'\btitles?\b|\bmetadata\b|\bsimilar\b|\bplausib|\blooks? like\b|self[- ]describ|\bassum', re.I)
624	
625	
626	def authentication_failure(basis: str) -> str:
627	    """'' if `basis` authenticates SPAN PRESERVATION; otherwise the reason it does not.
628	
629	    Sol, verbatim: "A title similarity match can PROPOSE predecessor_of; it CANNOT ASSERT
630	    exact_copy_of."
631	
632	    An ASSERTED span-preserving edge is the ONLY thing in this graph that lets a span name a document
633	    WHOSE BYTES WE DO NOT HOLD. It is therefore the only thing worth attacking, and it was guarded on
634	    ONE of its two types: the old check read `type == 'exact_copy_of' and 'title' in basis`. But
635	    `accepted_manuscript_of` is span-preserving too, and it widens attribution identically — so
636	
637	        add_edge(wp, journal, 'accepted_manuscript_of', 'ASSERTED', 'the titles match')
638	
639	    was ACCEPTED, and it made the working paper's spans journal-attributable. The rule is not "no
640	    title matches for exact_copy_of". The rule is: an ASSERTED span-preserving edge must name
641	    BYTE-LEVEL EVIDENCE, whatever it is called. Both directions are enforced here, in ONE predicate,
642	    used by `add_edge()` at construction AND by `from_json()` at load — because an edge that cannot be
643	    constructed but CAN BE LOADED is not a rule, it is a speed bump.
644	    """
645	    if not basis or not basis.strip():
646	        return 'an edge MUST carry a basis naming the evidence for it'
647	    weak = _PROVES_NOTHING_ABOUT_BYTES.search(basis)
648	    if weak:
649	        return (f'the basis rests on {weak.group(0)!r}, which proves NOTHING ABOUT BYTES — PEER REVIEW '
650	                f'CHANGES NUMBERS, and matching titles/metadata cannot show a span survived it')
651	    if not _AUTHENTICATES.search(basis):
652	        return ('the basis names no BYTE-LEVEL evidence (a byte-for-byte / checksum / span-level '
653	                'comparison against the target expression). Nothing else can ASSERT span preservation')
654	    return ''
655	
656	
657	@dataclass(frozen=True)
658	class SourcePolicy:
659	    """WHICH EXPRESSIONS THIS ANSWER IS ALLOWED TO CITE. The task's instruction, made mechanical.
660	
661	    This is an ARGUMENT, not a stored field, because admissibility is a property of THE ANSWER and not
662	    of the bytes. The same accepted manuscript is inadmissible under "journal articles only" and
663	    admissible under "any identified version"; a boolean on the node has to be wrong for one of them.
664	    `Expression.citable_in_journal_only_answer` was exactly that boolean, and it is gone.
665	    """
666	    name: str
667	    permitted_expression_kinds: tuple[str, ...]
668	    require_complete: bool = True
669	
670	
671	#: Task 72. "If only the working paper is available, citing it VIOLATES THE JOURNAL-ONLY INSTRUCTION."
672	JOURNAL_ONLY  = SourcePolicy('journal_articles_only', ('journal_version',))
673	PEER_REVIEWED = SourcePolicy('peer_reviewed', ('journal_version', 'proceedings_version'))
674	#: For a legal or clinical question, the authoritative text IS the source. There is no journal here.
675	OFFICIAL_TEXT = SourcePolicy('official_text_only', ('official_text', 'registry_record'))
676	ANY_VERSION   = SourcePolicy('any_identified_version',
677	                             ('journal_version', 'proceedings_version', 'accepted_manuscript',
678	                              'working_paper', 'preprint', 'official_text', 'registry_record'))
679	
680	
681	@dataclass(frozen=True)
682	class Attribution:
683	    """The resolved answer to: may a span from these bytes be attributed AT ALL, and TO WHAT?"""
684	    manifestation_id: str
685	    content_hash: str
686	    expression_id: str                        # the expression whose bytes these ARE
687	    permitted_expression_ids: tuple[str, ...]  # everything a span here may name (IDs — never prose)
688	    policy: str
689	    admitted: bool
690	    names_expression_id: str | None           # THE id the sentence must name. None => name NOTHING.
691	    text: str | None                          # display cache. NOTHING IS EVER VALIDATED AGAINST IT.
692	    refusal: str | None
693	
694	
695	@dataclass
696	class Graph:
697	    works: dict[str, Work] = field(default_factory=dict)
698	    expressions: dict[str, Expression] = field(default_factory=dict)
699	    manifestations: dict[str, Manifestation] = field(default_factory=dict)
700	    edges: list[Edge] = field(default_factory=list)
701	
702	    # -- construction -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
703	    def add_edge(self, src: str, dst: str, type: str, status: str, basis: str) -> Edge:
704	        if type not in EDGE_TYPES:
705	            raise ValueError(f'unknown edge type {type!r}')
706	        if status not in ('PROPOSED', 'ASSERTED'):
707	            raise ValueError(f'edge status must be PROPOSED or ASSERTED, not {status!r}')
708	        if not basis or not basis.strip():
709	            # An edge with no evidence is the entire failure we are here to end.
710	            raise ValueError('an edge MUST carry a basis naming the evidence for it')
711	        if status == 'ASSERTED' and type in SPAN_PRESERVING:
712	            # Enforced in the constructor so no caller can talk its way past it, and re-enforced in
713	            # from_json() so no FILE can either.
714	            why = authentication_failure(basis)
715	            if why:
716	                raise ValueError(f'cannot ASSERT {type} — {why}')
717	        e = Edge(src, dst, type, status, basis)
718	        self.edges.append(e)
719	        return e
720	
721	    # -- THE CORE RULE ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
722	    def attribution_targets(self, manifestation_id: str) -> list[str]:
723	        """Which expressions a span in this manifestation MAY name. Sol's rule, mechanised.
724	
725	        Always: the manifestation's own expression -- the bytes we hold are, definitionally, that
726	        expression's bytes. Additionally: any expression reachable by an ASSERTED, SPAN-PRESERVING
727	        edge, because such an edge is exactly a proof that the cited source contains the same span.
728	        Nothing else. A `predecessor_of` edge, however obvious, transfers NOTHING.
729	        """
730	        m = self.manifestations[manifestation_id]
731	        targets = [m.expression_id]
732	        for e in self.edges:
733	            if e.src == m.expression_id and e.type in SPAN_PRESERVING and e.status == 'ASSERTED':
734	                if e.dst not in targets:
735	                    targets.append(e.dst)
736	        return targets
737	
738	    # -- THE API. THREE FUNCTIONS. --------------------------------------------------------------
739	    def bind_span(self, manifestation_id: str, start: int, end: int) -> dict:
740	        """A span binds to its EXACT manifestation and content hash. This is what a card must carry
741	        instead of a bare DOI -- a DOI names a Work, and a Work has no bytes.
742	
743	        BOUNDS ARE VALIDATED HERE. THEY WERE NOT, AND PYTHON IS SILENT ABOUT NONSENSE: for a
744	        1,000-char text, `text[-5:20]`, `text[900:100]` and `text[5000:6000]` are all `''`. Every one
745	        of those is a span that BINDS CLEANLY AND THEN VERIFIES — its stored text is empty, the empty
746	        slice still equals it, and the content hash is perfectly correct. The card would name a real
747	        paper, carry a real hash, pass verify_span(), and quote NOTHING FROM IT. That is a fabricated
748	        citation with a valid receipt, which is the exact shape of every defect in this file's header.
749	        A negative offset is worse than empty: `text[-5:]` silently indexes from the END and binds a
750	        span from somewhere else entirely.
751	
752	        So they are REFUSED, not clamped. Clamping would invent a span the miner never mined.
753	
754	        RETURNS IDENTIFIERS, NOT PROSE. `may_name` used to hand back English SENTENCES, so a consumer
755	        deciding what was legal had to string-match a display string. Prose is a cache; an id resolves.
756	        """
757	        m = self.manifestations.get(manifestation_id)
758	        if m is None:
759	            raise SpanBindingError(f'no manifestation {manifestation_id!r} — a span cannot bind to '
760	                                   f'bytes we do not hold')
761	        for nm, v in (('start', start), ('end', end)):
762	            if isinstance(v, bool) or not isinstance(v, int):
763	                raise SpanBindingError(f'{nm} offset must be an int, got {v!r} ({type(v).__name__})')
764	        n = len(m.text)
765	        if start < 0 or end < 0:
766	            raise SpanBindingError(
767	                f'NEGATIVE span [{start}:{end}] on {manifestation_id} — Python would index from the '
768	                f'END of the text and bind a span from a different part of the document')
769	        if end <= start:
770	            raise SpanBindingError(
771	                f'EMPTY OR REVERSED span [{start}:{end}] on {manifestation_id} — it slices to "" and '
772	                f'would then verify perfectly against nothing, under a real citation')
773	        if end > n:
774	            raise SpanBindingError(
775	                f'span [{start}:{end}] runs past the end of {manifestation_id} ({n} chars) — Python '
776	                f'would truncate it silently, and the stored text would not be the span that was mined')
777	        return dict(
778	            manifestation_id=manifestation_id,
779	            content_hash=m.content_hash,
780	            span_start=start, span_end=end,
781	            text=m.text[start:end],
782	            expression_id=m.expression_id,
783	            permitted_expression_ids=list(self.attribution_targets(manifestation_id)),
784	        )
785	
786	    def resolve_attribution(self, manifestation_id: str,
787	                            source_policy: SourcePolicy = JOURNAL_ONLY) -> Attribution:
788	        """MAY a span from these bytes be attributed under THIS task's instruction, and TO WHAT ID?
789	
790	        Two independent conditions, BOTH required:
791	          1. the bytes are usable as evidence at all — complete, uncorrupted, the right work; AND
792	          2. some expression the span MAY LEGALLY NAME (own expression, or one across an ASSERTED
793	             span-preserving edge) is of a kind THIS POLICY PERMITS.
794	
795	        A span from a corrupt PDF of the real journal article is still inadmissible -- see Weiss, whose
796	        verbatim span said "633 per cent" because the PDF was a Caesar-shifted encoding of "300".
797	        """
798	        m = self.manifestations.get(manifestation_id)
799	        if m is None:
800	            raise KeyError(f'no manifestation {manifestation_id!r}')
801	        targets = tuple(self.attribution_targets(manifestation_id))
802	        base = dict(manifestation_id=manifestation_id, content_hash=m.content_hash,
803	                    expression_id=m.expression_id, permitted_expression_ids=targets,
804	                    policy=source_policy.name)
805	
806	        if source_policy.require_complete and not m.profile.get('complete'):
807	            why = '; '.join(m.profile.get('incomplete_because') or ['the bytes are not a usable document'])
808	            return Attribution(**base, admitted=False, names_expression_id=None, text=None,
809	                               refusal=f'these bytes may not carry a finding: {why}')
810	
811	        # Prefer the expression whose bytes these ACTUALLY ARE. Widening is a fallback, never a first
812	        # choice: naming the document we hold is always the strongest true thing we can say.
813	        permitted = [t for t in targets
814	                     if self.expressions[t].kind in source_policy.permitted_expression_kinds]
815	        if not permitted:
816	            kinds = ', '.join(sorted({self.expressions[t].kind for t in targets})) or 'nothing'
817	            return Attribution(
818	                **base, admitted=False, names_expression_id=None, text=None,
819	                refusal=(f'policy `{source_policy.name}` permits '
820	                         f'{{{", ".join(source_policy.permitted_expression_kinds)}}}, and a span here '
821	                         f'may only name {{{kinds}}}. No ASSERTED span-preserving edge widens it — '
822	                         f'citing a permitted source with THESE bytes would be fabrication'))
823	        chosen = m.expression_id if m.expression_id in permitted else permitted[0]
824	        return Attribution(**base, admitted=True, names_expression_id=chosen,
825	                           text=self.expressions[chosen].attribution, refusal=None)
826	
827	    def verify_span(self, binding: dict) -> bool:
828	        """The enforcement point. A binding arriving here is AN UNTRUSTED INPUT — by now it has been
829	        through a JSON file — so this re-checks EVERYTHING bind_span() checked, and then the bytes.
830	
831	        A gate that trusts the offsets it is handed is a gate that verifies `text[900:100] == ''`.
832	        """
833	        try:
834	            mid = binding['manifestation_id']
835	            s, e = binding['span_start'], binding['span_end']
836	            stored, chash = binding['text'], binding['content_hash']
837	        except (KeyError, TypeError):
838	            return False
839	        m = self.manifestations.get(mid)
840	        if m is None or m.content_hash != chash:
841	            return False
842	        for v in (s, e):
843	            if isinstance(v, bool) or not isinstance(v, int):
844	                return False
845	        if s < 0 or e <= s or e > len(m.text):
846	            return False
847	        # the node itself must not have been edited since it was loaded: the hash is over THE TEXT.
848	        if hashlib.sha256(m.text.encode('utf-8', 'ignore')).hexdigest() != m.content_hash:
849	            return False
850	        if m.text[s:e] != stored:
851	            return False
852	        # ...and the permitted set is not a cache. If an edge changed, the STORED permission is stale,
853	        # and a stale permission is how a span keeps naming a journal it was never allowed to name.
854	        if 'permitted_expression_ids' in binding:
855	            if list(binding['permitted_expression_ids']) != list(self.attribution_targets(mid)):
856	                return False
857	        return True
858	
859	    # -- derived conveniences (they go THROUGH the API above; they are not a second door) ----------
860	    def journal_attributable(self, manifestation_id: str) -> bool:
861	        """May a span from these bytes be printed as a finding OF A JOURNAL ARTICLE? (task 72)"""
862	        return self.resolve_attribution(manifestation_id, JOURNAL_ONLY).admitted
863	
864	    # -- serialisation ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
865	    def to_json(self) -> dict:
866	        return dict(
867	            works=[asdict(w) for w in self.works.values()],
868	            expressions=[asdict(e) for e in self.expressions.values()],
869	            # text is retained on disk too: NOTHING IS EVER DELETED.
870	            manifestations=[asdict(m) for m in self.manifestations.values()],
871	            edges=[asdict(e) for e in self.edges],
872	        )
873	
874	    @classmethod
875	    def from_json(cls, data: dict | str) -> 'Graph':
876	        """STRICT LOAD. A GRAPH ON DISK IS AN UNTRUSTED INPUT.
877	
878	        Every check here exists because the file it reads is the same KIND of artifact that lied to us
879	        already. `to_json()` writes the text, its hash, its word count and its derived profile, and any
880	        copy of that file — hand-edited, half-written, merged, or produced by an older version of this
881	        module — can disagree with itself in every one of those places. A loader that accepts what it
882	        is handed re-opens the hole FROM THE OTHER SIDE: the enforcement point would read a
883	        `complete: true` that no bytes support, and would enforce it.
884	
885	        REFUSES, NEVER REPAIRS:
886	          - a content_hash that is not sha256(text)                  [the span gate's only anchor]
887	          - an n_words that is not len(text.split())                 [the count that describes a
888	                                                                       document we no longer hold —
889	                                                                       the exact defect event_ledger
890	                                                                       catches in the live corpus]
891	          - a stored extractability verdict the text does not support
892	          - a `complete: true` (or false) THE REGISTRY DOES NOT DERIVE for that kind and that text
893	          - a manifestation naming an expression or a work that does not exist
894	          - a manifestation whose expression belongs to a DIFFERENT work
895	          - an expression naming a work that does not exist
896	          - an edge whose endpoints do not exist, or that carries no basis
897	          - an ASSERTED span-preserving edge whose basis does not AUTHENTICATE span preservation
898	          - any unknown field, anywhere (a schema drift silently drops the field a gate reads)
899	        """
900	        if isinstance(data, str):
901	            data = json.loads(data)
902	        g = cls()
903	        err: list[str] = []

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""EVENT LEDGER — every status is a PURE REDUCER over an append-only event log.
3	
4	    Sol, item 8: "NO COMPONENT MAY WRITE `complete`, `fulltext`, `no evidence`, `same work`,
5	                  or `high quality` DIRECTLY."
6	
7	THE FAILURE THIS IS THE STRUCTURAL CURE FOR
8	
9	  Every defect we have shipped had ONE shape: A LABEL THAT ASSERTED MORE THAN ITS CONTENT
10	  SUPPORTED, WITH NOTHING CHECKING. Not one announced itself; each read as a fact about the world.
11	
12	      "gate: WIRED"          -> it checked the wrong lane; fabrication shipped
13	      "span-verified"        -> verified by its FIRST 60 CHARACTERS
14	      "still paywalled"      -> we asked by DOI; the free copy is a SEPARATE WORK
15	      "FULLTEXT"             -> 535 words. A cookie banner.
16	      "no free copy exists"  -> we were HTTP 429/404. A fact about OUR REQUEST and THEIR INDEX,
17	                                disguised as a fact about the world.
18	      "working_paper"        -> written by whichever SCRIPT ran, not by what the document IS.
19	
20	  A component that CAN assert its own success will eventually assert it falsely, and nothing will
21	  catch it. So here a component may ONLY EMIT EVENTS — observations, in the vocabulary of the world:
22	  an HTTP status, a word count, a byline, a glyph ratio. The LABEL is DERIVED from those events by a
23	  reducer nobody can bypass, and the label is never stored anywhere a component could overwrite it.
24	
25	THE TWO RULES THAT MAKE IT STRUCTURAL, NOT ADVISORY
26	
27	  1. EMIT-TIME CONCLUSION GUARD. `Ledger.emit()` REJECTS any event whose payload carries a terminal
28	     conclusion — a key like `content_status`, or a value like "FULLTEXT" / "same work" /
29	     "no evidence" / "high quality". You cannot write the label into the ledger even if you try.
30	     Raw text quoted from the world (`header_text`, `span`, `document_title`) is exempt: a fetched
31	     PDF is allowed to contain the word "complete". A COMPONENT is not.
32	
33	  2. LABELS ARE NEVER STORED. They are computed on read by `derive_*` pure functions over the event
34	     list. There is no field to corrupt, no cache to go stale, and no way to "just set it".
35	
36	THE DISTINCTIONS THAT MUST NEVER COLLAPSE (Sol)
37	
38	    HTTP 429/503        -> BACKEND_FAILED.  ** NOT "no evidence exists". **
39	    HTTP 404            -> NOT_INDEXED_BY_THIS_BACKEND. A fact about THEIR INDEX. Also not absence.
40	    route_complete      -> EVERY PLANNED ADAPTER HAS A TERMINAL OUTCOME RECORD.
41	                           It must NEVER mean "an adapter was mapped."
42	    a budget stop       -> IS NOT AN EVIDENCE GAP.
43	    absence             -> only an ADEQUATE route (every adapter genuinely answered, none throttled,
44	                           none blocked, no budget stop) can support a scoped absence statement.
45	    "high_quality"      -> FORBIDDEN as a bare label. It renders as its COMPONENTS, each with
46	                           provenance: directness=high, method_quality=low, influence_percentile=0.92.
47	
48	Run it:
49	    python3 scripts/event_ledger.py            # self-test + replay of our real history
50	    python3 scripts/event_ledger.py --replay   # just the replay
51	    python3 scripts/event_ledger.py --selftest # just the invariants
52	"""
53	from __future__ import annotations
54	
55	import json
56	import re
57	import sys
58	import time
59	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
60	from pathlib import Path
61	from typing import Any, Iterable
62	
63	ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
64	sys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).resolve().parent))
65	
66	# THE REGISTRY, AND THE ONE COMPLETENESS REDUCER. Imported — never re-implemented. This module used to
67	# carry its own `FULLTEXT_MIN = 2500` while provenance.py used 1,200, and neither knew about the
68	# other. See the headstone at "3. content profile", below.
69	from provenance import (  # noqa: E402
70	    KIND_PROFILE, judge_completeness,
71	    SOURCE_TYPE as _SOURCE_TYPE, WORK_KIND_ARTIFACT as _WORK_KIND_ARTIFACT,
72	)

105	class ForbiddenLabel(Exception):
106	    """A component tried to write a conclusion into the ledger. That is the whole disease."""

109	#: Terminal conclusions. A component may not state these — the reducer derives them.
110	RESERVED_VALUES = (
111	    'fulltext', 'full text', 'full-text',
112	    'complete', 'route_complete',
113	    'no evidence', 'no free copy', 'no free text', 'not available', 'nothing found',
114	    'same work', 'same_work', 'version equivalent',
115	    'high quality', 'high_quality', 'low quality', 'low_quality',
116	    'paywalled', 'still paywalled',
117	    'supported', 'searched_none', 'conflicted',
118	    'admissible', 'inadmissible', 'eligible',
119	    'verified', 'span-verified', 'fabrication-proof',
120	)
121	
122	#: Keys that ARE labels. Naming one is an attempt to set the label directly.
123	RESERVED_KEYS = (
124	    'content_status', 'status', 'label', 'verdict', 'quality', 'tier', 'grade',
125	    'fulltext_source', 'is_fulltext', 'route_complete', 'complete', 'coverage',
126	    'coverage_status', 'eligibility', 'binding', 'admissible', 'evidence_status',
127	)
128	
129	#: Fields that are RAW TEXT QUOTED FROM THE WORLD. A PDF may contain any word at all; that is not
130	#: the component asserting it. These are exempt from the value scan (never from the key scan).
131	#: NOTE what is NOT here: `note`. A free-text `note` is the COMPONENT talking, not the world, and it
132	#: is precisely where a conclusion would be smuggled in ("note: no free copy exists"). It stays
133	#: scanned. (The guard caught exactly that in this file's own replay code, and the fix was to reword
134	#: the note into an observation — not to exempt the field.)
135	#: `locator` is here for the same reason `url` is: IT IS A URL. Publishers put our own reserved words
136	#: inside their paths (`/articles/PMC.../?report=fulltext`, `/science/article/.../fulltext`), and a
137	#: guard that refused to record THE ADDRESS THE BYTES CAME FROM — because the address contains the
138	#: string "fulltext" — would have made the locator unrecordable and left us exactly where we were:
139	#: holding an NBER working paper under a URL pointing at aeaweb.org.
140	#: `requested_venue` sits beside `requested_title` for the same reason: it is a NAME, quoted from the
141	#: bibliography. There is a journal called `Information & Management` and there could be one called
142	#: `Complete Systems`; a guard that refused to record which journal we asked about, because the
143	#: journal's name contains one of our reserved words, would be a guard against nothing.
144	VERBATIM_KEYS = frozenset({
145	    'header_text', 'identity_window', 'span', 'document_title', 'byline', 'snippet', 'raw', 'title',
146	    'reason_text', 'requested_title', 'requested_venue', 'url', 'locator', 'doi', 'venue',
147	})

150	def _scan_value(k: str, v, bad: list[str], path: str = '') -> None:
151	    """Scan ONE value, RECURSIVELY.
152	
153	    IT DID NOT RECURSE. The scan ran over `payload.items()` and tested `isinstance(v, str)` — so a
154	    conclusion one level down was invisible to it:
155	
156	        emit(..., adapter_observations={'content_status': 'FULLTEXT'})    # ACCEPTED. Silently.
157	
158	    A guard that only inspects the top level of a nested structure is a guard you step over by adding
159	    a dict, and "the checks certify a lane the fabrication no longer uses" is the sentence this whole
160	    project is trying to stop writing. Payloads stay flat by convention; this makes it true by force.
161	    """
162	    where = f'{path}{k}'
163	    if isinstance(v, str):
164	        vl = v.lower().strip()
165	        for r in RESERVED_VALUES:
166	            # match the whole value or a word-bounded occurrence — not a substring of a word
167	            if vl == r or re.search(rf'(?<![a-z0-9_]){re.escape(r)}(?![a-z0-9_])', vl):
168	                bad.append(f'{where}={v!r} states the conclusion {r!r}; emit the OBSERVATION instead '
169	                           f'(word_count / http_status / byline), and let the reducer conclude')
170	                return
171	    elif isinstance(v, dict):
172	        for k2, v2 in v.items():
173	            if str(k2).lower() in RESERVED_KEYS:
174	                bad.append(f'key {where}.{k2!r} IS a label — the reducer derives it; a component may '
175	                           f'not set it, and burying it one level down does not make it an observation')
176	                continue
177	            if str(k2).lower() in VERBATIM_KEYS:
178	                continue
179	            _scan_value(str(k2), v2, bad, path=f'{where}.')
180	    elif isinstance(v, (list, tuple)):
181	        for i, v2 in enumerate(v):
182	            _scan_value(f'[{i}]', v2, bad, path=where)
183

185	@dataclass(frozen=True)
186	class Event:
187	    """An OBSERVATION. Immutable, ordered, and never a conclusion."""
188	    seq: int
189	    unit: str                    # what this is about — a DOI, a coverage cell id, a route id
190	    kind: str
191	    actor: str                   # which component observed it (for provenance, not for authority)
192	    payload: dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
193	    ts: float = field(default_factory=time.time)
194	
195	    def to_json(self) -> str:
196	        return json.dumps({'seq': self.seq, 'unit': self.unit, 'kind': self.kind,
197	                           'actor': self.actor, 'payload': self.payload, 'ts': self.ts},
198	                          sort_keys=True)

201	def _scan_for_conclusions(kind: str, payload: dict) -> list[str]:
202	    """Return the reasons this payload is an ASSERTION rather than an OBSERVATION."""
203	    bad: list[str] = []
204	    for k, v in payload.items():
205	        kl = str(k).lower()
206	        if kl in RESERVED_KEYS:
207	            bad.append(f'key {k!r} IS a label — the reducer derives it; a component may not set it')
208	            continue
209	        if kl in VERBATIM_KEYS:
210	            continue                       # raw text from the world. It may say anything.
211	        _scan_value(str(k), v, bad)
212	    return bad

215	def _observations(events: list['Event']) -> list['Event']:
216	    """THE OBSERVATIONS ONLY. A reducer NEVER reads another reducer's recorded verdict.
217	
218	    `record_derivation()` writes audit artifacts into the same log, and the module docstring has
219	    always claimed "NO REDUCER READS THEM". That was true only BY ACCIDENT OF KIND CHOICE — nothing
220	    enforced it. It was one line from being false in the worst way: a derived record written with
221	    kind=CONTENT_PROFILE_DERIVED is picked up by `derive_content_profile`'s `for e in events` loop,
222	    which keeps the LAST match — so the reducer would have read ITS OWN PREVIOUS OUTPUT as if it were
223	    an observation of the bytes, and the label would have become a cache of itself. That is the cookie
224	    banner, again, from the inside.
225	
226	    So the invariant is now structural, in one place, and every derive_*() below goes through it.
227	    """
228	    return [e for e in events if 'derived_by' not in e.payload]

231	class LedgerCorrupt(Exception):
232	    """The persisted log does not agree with itself. It is REFUSED, never silently repaired."""
233	
234	
235	class Ledger:
236	    """Append-only, DURABLE, and RELOADABLE. You may add an observation. You may not edit, delete,
237	    or conclude.
238	
239	    IT DID NOT RELOAD. `__init__` set `self._events = []` and never read the file, so a ledger opened
240	    over an existing JSONL began with an EMPTY HISTORY — while still appending to the end of it. Every
241	    standalone script therefore reduced over the handful of events IT had emitted, not over what
242	    happened. `derive_route_status()` would find one adapter planned instead of four and call the route
243	    COMPLETE; `derive_coverage_status()` would license a scoped absence off a history it could not see.
244	    A reducer over an append-only log that does not read the log is not a reducer over anything — and
245	    it fails in the direction of CONFIDENCE, which is the only direction that ships.
246	    """
247	
248	    def __init__(self, path: Path | str | None = None, *, load: bool = True):
249	        self._events: list[Event] = []
250	        self._path = Path(path) if path else None
251	        self._seq = 0
252	        if self._path is not None:
253	            self._path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
254	            if load and self._path.exists():
255	                self._load()
256	
257	    @classmethod
258	    def load(cls, path: Path | str) -> 'Ledger':
259	        """Open the ledger AS IT STANDS ON DISK. This is the constructor a standalone script wants."""
260	        return cls(path, load=True)
261	
262	    # -- read the history back --------------------------------------------------------------------
263	    def _load(self) -> None:
264	        """A PERSISTED LEDGER IS AN UNTRUSTED INPUT, and it is exactly as untrusted as the corpus was.
265	
266	        The emit-time guard is RE-APPLIED here. Bypassing it on read would leave the door it locks
267	        standing open: `echo '{"payload":{"content_status":"FULLTEXT"}}' >> ledger.jsonl` is not a
268	        harder attack than calling emit(), it is an easier one.
269	
270	        A record written by a reducer (it carries `derived_by`) is exempt from that scan, because its
271	        whole job is to hold a derived verdict. Forging one buys nothing: NO REDUCER READS THEM. They
272	        are audit artifacts, and every derive_*() in this file re-derives from the OBSERVATIONS. That
273	        is the difference between a ledger and a cache, and caching the label is how we shipped
274	        FULLTEXT on a cookie banner.
275	        """
276	        seen: set[int] = set()
277	        events: list[Event] = []
278	        for i, line in enumerate(self._path.read_text().splitlines(), 1):
279	            line = line.strip()
280	            if not line:
281	                continue
282	            where = f'{self._path.name}:{i}'
283	            try:
284	                d = json.loads(line)
285	            except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
286	                raise LedgerCorrupt(f'{where}: not JSON — {e}')
287	            missing = {'seq', 'unit', 'kind', 'actor', 'payload', 'ts'} - set(d)
288	            if missing:
289	                raise LedgerCorrupt(f'{where}: missing field(s) {sorted(missing)}')
290	            if d['kind'] not in EventKind.ALL:
291	                raise LedgerCorrupt(f'{where}: unknown event kind {d["kind"]!r}')
292	            if not isinstance(d['payload'], dict):
293	                raise LedgerCorrupt(f'{where}: payload is not an object')
294	            if not isinstance(d['seq'], int) or d['seq'] in seen:
295	                raise LedgerCorrupt(f'{where}: seq {d["seq"]!r} is duplicated or not an integer — an '
296	                                    f'append-only log with a repeated sequence number has been edited')
297	            if 'derived_by' not in d['payload']:
298	                problems = _scan_for_conclusions(d['kind'], d['payload'])
299	                if problems:
300	                    raise LedgerCorrupt(
301	                        f'{where}: this line writes a CONCLUSION into the ledger — the emit guard '
302	                        f'refuses it, and so does the loader:\n    ' + '\n    '.join(problems))
303	            seen.add(d['seq'])
304	            events.append(Event(seq=d['seq'], unit=d['unit'], kind=d['kind'], actor=d['actor'],
305	                                payload=d['payload'], ts=d['ts']))
306	        events.sort(key=lambda e: e.seq)
307	        self._events = events
308	        self._seq = max(seen, default=0)     # ...so the next append CONTINUES the log, never collides
309	
310	    # -- write side: exactly one method, and it is guarded -------------------------------------
311	    def emit(self, unit: str, kind: str, actor: str, **payload) -> Event:
312	        if kind not in EventKind.ALL:
313	            raise ForbiddenLabel(f'unknown event kind {kind!r}')
314	        problems = _scan_for_conclusions(kind, payload)
315	        if problems:
316	            raise ForbiddenLabel(
317	                f'{actor} tried to write a CONCLUSION into the ledger:\n    ' + '\n    '.join(problems))
318	        return self._append(unit, kind, actor, payload)
319	
320	    def _append(self, unit: str, kind: str, actor: str, payload: dict) -> Event:
321	        self._seq += 1
322	        ev = Event(seq=self._seq, unit=unit, kind=kind, actor=actor, payload=dict(payload))
323	        self._events.append(ev)
324	        if self._path:
325	            # One line, one open, closed immediately: the record is with the OS before emit() returns.
326	            # An event that is in memory but not on disk is a history that disagrees with itself the
327	            # moment the process dies.
328	            with open(self._path, 'a') as fh:
329	                fh.write(ev.to_json() + '\n')
330	                fh.flush()
331	        return ev
332	
333	    # -- the reducer's audit trail --------------------------------------------------------------
334	    def record_derivation(self, unit: str, kind: str, reducer: str, inputs: list[int],
335	                          **verdict) -> Event:
336	        """A reducer may append its own verdict — FOR AUDIT ONLY. Nobody else can call this.
337	
338	        The frame check below is not decoration. `emit()` is the door components use, and it refuses
339	        conclusions. This is the door the reducer uses, and it is bolted from the inside: if the
340	        calling frame is not this module, it raises. There is no third door.
341	
342	        Downstream reducers NEVER TRUST these records — they RE-DERIVE from the observations. A
343	        recorded verdict is an audit artifact, not an authority. That is the difference between a
344	        ledger and a cache, and caching the label is how we shipped "FULLTEXT" on a cookie banner.
345	        """
346	        import inspect
347	        caller = inspect.currentframe().f_back
348	        if caller.f_globals.get('__name__') != __name__:
349	            raise ForbiddenLabel(
350	                f'{caller.f_globals.get("__name__")!r} tried to record a DERIVED label directly. '
351	                f'Only a reducer inside {__name__} may. Emit OBSERVATIONS and call derive_*().')
352	        return self._append(unit, kind, reducer,
353	                            {**verdict, 'derived_by': reducer, 'from_events': list(inputs)})
354	
355	    # -- read side ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
356	    def events(self, unit: str | None = None, kind: str | None = None) -> list[Event]:
357	        return [e for e in self._events
358	                if (unit is None or e.unit == unit) and (kind is None or e.kind == kind)]
359	
360	    def units(self) -> list[str]:
361	        return list(dict.fromkeys(e.unit for e in self._events))
362	
363	    def __len__(self) -> int:
364	        return len(self._events)
365

368	# OBSERVERS — pure functions from raw content to OBSERVATIONS. They conclude nothing.

370	
371	_CID = re.compile(r'\(cid:\d+\)')
372	_CHROME = re.compile(
373	    r'website uses cookies|accept\s+(?:all\s+)?cookies|by clicking the "accept" button|'
374	    r'enable javascript|sign in to (?:your account|continue)|create an account|'
375	    r'this site requires|subscribe to (?:read|view)|purchase access|add to cart|'
376	    # ...the repository LANDING PAGES that fooled us: Oxford ORA, PubMed Central, generic portals.
377	    r'skip to main content|we welcome feedback|send message|research archive|'
378	    r'privacy policy|terms of use|all rights reserved|official websites use',
379	    re.I)
380	_PUBLISHED_STAMP = re.compile(r'published version|version of record|\(refereed\)|original citation', re.I)
381	_PREPRINT_STAMP = re.compile(
382	    r'nber working paper (?:series|no)|national bureau of economic research|'
383	    r'iza (?:dp|discussion paper)|this (?:draft|version):|preliminary(?: and incomplete)?|'
384	    r'do not (?:cite|quote) without|arxiv:\d', re.I)

387	def observe_text(text: str, header_chars: int = 1500) -> dict:
388	    """Everything the ledger needs to know about a blob of retrieved text — and NOT ONE CONCLUSION.
389	
390	    Note `header_chars`: a preprint stamp is only evidence of what a document IS when it appears in
391	    the document's OWN header. Every economics paper cites NBER working papers in its REFERENCES —
392	    scanning the whole text for "NBER Working Paper" identifies the bibliography, not the paper.
393	    (That naive scan called the actual Journal of Economic Perspectives article a working paper.)
394	    """
395	    words = text.split()
396	    header = text[:header_chars]
397	    cid_tokens = sum(1 for t in words if 'cid:' in t)
398	    stripped = _CID.sub('', text)
399	    real_words = len(re.findall(r'[A-Za-z]{3,}', stripped))
400	    # How much REAL text is in the header? A title page of pure (cid:NN) glyph codes decodes to
401	    # nothing — and a header we cannot READ is a header we cannot CHECK AN IDENTITY AGAINST. It is
402	    # not a stranger's paper. It is an unreadable one, and those are different facts.
403	    header_real = len(re.findall(r'[A-Za-z]{3,}', _CID.sub('', header)))
404	    return {
405	        'word_count': len(words),
406	        'char_count': len(text),
407	        'readable_word_count': real_words,
408	        'glyph_garbage_ratio': round(cid_tokens / max(1, len(words)), 4),
409	        'chrome_markers': len(_CHROME.findall(text)),
410	        'published_stamp_in_header': bool(_PUBLISHED_STAMP.search(header)),
411	        'preprint_stamp_in_header': bool(_PREPRINT_STAMP.search(header)),
412	        'preprint_stamp_anywhere': bool(_PREPRINT_STAMP.search(text)),
413	        'header_real_words': header_real,
414	        # the window identity is checked in. BOUNDED ON PURPOSE: searching the WHOLE document for an
415	        # author name matches the REFERENCES — every requested author is "present" somewhere in every
416	        # paper. (A mathematician named Parry is cited in the maths paper we misfiled under Parry
417	        # et al. A whole-text search "confirmed" the wrong paper.)
418	        'identity_window': header,
419	        'header_text': header[:400],
420	    }
421
